channel.title: Ramah: Good News Feed
channel.description: Positive news stories from around the world, filtered by sentiment analysis

item.title: TikTok and Meta risked safety to win algorithm arms race, whistleblowers say
item.description: Social media giants made decisions which allowed more harmful content on people's feeds, after internal research into their algorithms showed how outrage fuelled engagement, whistleblowers told the BBC.

item.title: Petrol is running out! Is it time to panic?! Help help | First Dog on the Moon
item.description: The government is asking people to remain calm which is always a sign that it is time to flip out massivelySign up here to get an email whenever First Dog cartoons are publishedGet all your needs met at the First Dog shop if what you need is First Dog merchandise and prints Continue reading.

item.title: 5 takeaways from an Oscars night that spread the love
item.description: As Sunday's Oscars ceremony approached, it seemed to be shaping up to be a showdown between the vampires and the revolutionaries, between Sinners and One Battle After Another.

item.title: ‘Next year, it’s going to be a Waymo in a tux’: the best quotes from Oscars 2026
item.description: Hathaway: Anna, just curious, what do you think of my dress tonight?.

item.title: New rock monitor lizard species reveals remarkable evolutionary story
item.description: Three new species of rock monitor lizards previously unknown to science have been formally described in Far North Queensland.

item.title: Historic firsts, risqué jokes, and a tie - watch the top moments
item.description: The 2026 Oscars featured surprise wins, emotional speeches and some comical moments as stars gathered for Hollywood's biggest night.

item.title: One Battle After Another sweeps the Oscars as Michael B Jordan and Jessie Buckley win big
item.description: Paul Thomas Anderson’s counter-culture caper One Battle After Another has won the Oscars war, taking home six awards after a hotly contested season.

item.title: Best animated film winner Kpop Demon Hunters captures hearts world over
item.description: Kpop Demon Hunters has won best animated film at the Oscars on Sunday evening - the latest in a growing list of accolades for the movie that has swept the world since it was released last summer.

item.title: Sinners’ Autumn Durald Arkapaw becomes first woman – and first black person – to win best cinematography Oscar
item.description: Sinners’ director of photography Autumn Durald Arkapaw has become the first woman to win the Oscar for best cinematography at the 98th Academy Awards.

item.title: Mr Nobody Against Putin wins the best documentary Oscar
item.description: Mr Nobody Against Putin, a primary school teacher’s record of the indoctrination of his pupils to support Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, has won the Oscar for best documentary.

item.title: One-off £2,000 grant gives care leavers head start, study finds
item.description: Care leavers who were given a one-off, no strings attached £2,000 grant were less likely to become homeless or spend time in hospital or prison, according to a new study.

item.title: Alex Johnston bedlam delivers one of rugby league’s most unforgettable nights | Jack Snape
item.description: Alex Johnston is adored by all in rugby league, but even so, this was extreme.

item.title: Untagged livestock roaming free continues to raise alarm across WA shires
item.description: A farmer in Western Australia's south has joined growing calls for stronger action to address wandering livestock on rural properties and roads.

item.title: Jannik Sinnner ends wait for title with Indian Wells win over Daniil Medvedev
item.description: Jannik Sinner claimed his first title of the year with victory over Daniil Medvedev in Indian Wells, while Aryna Sabalenka snapped her losing streak against Elena Rybakina in a thrilling women’s final.

item.title: Taxpayer bill for saving Scunthorpe steel furnaces could top £1.5bn by 2028, auditor says
item.description: The cost of keeping the UK’s last remaining blast furnaces going at British Steel’s Scunthorpe plant could exceed £1.5bn by 2028 if it continues at its current rate, according to the government’s spending watchdog.

item.title: Oscars 2026: Winners list in full (updating live)
item.description: Hollywood's best and brightest have gathered to find out which of them will be taking home one of the most coveted awards in the movie industry, the Oscars.

item.title: Oscars 2026 winners list: Michael B Jordan takes Best Actor, One Battle After Another wins Best Picture
item.description: Hollywood's night of night has taken place this evening with One Battle After Another dominating.

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' takes best picture. Here's the full list of Oscar winners
item.description: One Battle After Another took home best picture at the 98th Oscars on Sunday night.

item.title: Amy Madigan wins best supporting actress Oscar for Weapons
item.description: Amy Madigan has won the best supporting actress Oscar for Weapons at the 98th Academy Awards,.

item.title: First-round of French local elections sees strong showing for National Rally and LFI
item.description: The first-round of the French municipal elections have seen a strong showing for Marine Le Pen’s far-right the National Rally (RN), as well as for Jean-Luc Mélenchon’s radical left, with both parties likely to increase their local presence ahead of next year’s French presidential race.

item.title: Live: Oscars underway as Amy Madigan and KPop Demon Hunters win early
item.description: The 98th Academy Awards are here! Australia's Rose Byrne and Jacob Elordi are in the running to win their first Oscars and potentially cap off an already successful awards season with the top honours.

item.title: Oscars 2026: the red carpet, the ceremony, the winners – follow the action live!
item.description: Meanwhile, Peter Bradshaw’s red carpet whirlwind continues apace.

item.title: See the full list: Here are the winners of the 2026 Oscars
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: How this Sydney high school drastically improved their HSC results
item.description: When Janice Neilsen joined South Sydney High School as principal seven years ago, turning around their HSC results was a top priority.

item.title: 'The country is watching': Bernardi says One Nation must win seats in SA poll
item.description: One Nation's performance in recent polling has been one of the prominent themes of the SA election campaign.

item.title: 'A dream come true': What life is like a year after The Piano
item.description: For 14-year-old Melburnian Stefania Robu, walking down the street has become a "bizarre experience".

item.title: Chelsea’s James leads way to win League Cup final against Manchester United
item.description: Death, taxes and Chelsea women winning trophies.

item.title: Manchester United sink Aston Villa to tighten grip on Champions League spot
item.description: On 71 minutes a classic Manchester United riposte, via Matheus Cunha, to Ross Barkley’s equaliser moments before.

item.title: UK looking at all options to secure Strait of Hormuz, says Miliband
item.description: The UK is looking at "any options" - including working with the US and allies - to secure key oil shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz, Ed Miliband has said.

item.title: How a ‘vacuum cleaner turned the other way’ became a popular solution to snoring disorders
item.description: When Nick went camping in the summer with friends, he would set up his tent 100 metres away from the group.

item.title: Grounds for optimism at North Melbourne as emerging talents give glimpse of rosy future | Jonathan Horn
item.description: For most of his tenure at North Melbourne, Alastair Clarkson’s eyebrows have been arched in a kind of perma-frown.

item.title: Prue Leith looks back: ‘I had a great time on Bake Off, but I don’t think I’ll have any yearning when I see Nigella in that position’
item.description: Born in Cape Town in 1940, Prue Leith is a restaurateur, chef, broadcaster and writer.

item.title: The kindness of strangers: on an emptied train carriage, a man rubbed his hand on my thigh – then another passenger intervened
item.description: It was already dark when I boarded the train home.

item.title: Readers reply: which are more like life, novels or films?
item.description: This week’s question: travel broadens the mind – what other sayings are patently false?.

item.title: Mikaela Shiffrin closes on overall World Cup title after record-equaling slalom win
item.description: Olympic champion Mikaela Shiffrin won her record-equaling eighth World Cup slalom of the season on Sunday but her main rival in the overall standings was second to maintain pressure on the American star.

item.title: Olympic champion Shiffrin takes record 8th slalom win of season but main rival Aicher is 2nd
item.description: ÅRE, Sweden (AP) — After crossing the finish line for her record-equaling eighth World Cup slalom win of the season on Sunday, Olympic champion Mikaela Shiffrin bent over and let out a loud scream.

item.title: I love vultures, mosquitoes and, yes, even wasps. This is why you should too | Jo Wimpenny
item.description: A wasp has just flown into your kitchen.

item.title: Nathan Martin wins closest-ever Los Angeles marathon in its 40-year history
item.description: In the final moments of the Los Angeles marathon last weekend, the announcers were already narrating frontrunner Michael Kimani Kamau’s finish when Nathan Martin suddenly propelled forward, shocking newscasters and spectators.

item.title: Peter Bradshaw’s Oscars 2026 predictions: who will win, who should win, who should’ve been in the running?
item.description: Will Sinners and One Battle After Another sweep the board as expected, or are there surprises on the way? Our critic shares his predictions for the big-screen triumphs and disasters• Join us from 8pm GMT for live coverageWill win: SinnersShould win: The Secret AgentShoulda been a contender: The Mastermind Continue reading.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli takes his first F1 win with victory in China to extend Mercedes’ dominant start
item.description: SHANGHAI (AP) — He’s been defined by being Lewis Hamilton’s successor and George Russell’s teammate.

item.title: This is how we do it: ‘We’re more adventurous now – I’ve discovered my animalistic side’
item.description: Eva and I have been together for four years, but we lived in different countries for two of those years, seeing each other every other weekend.

item.title: Irresistible Japan cruises into Asian Cup semis and delights diaspora
item.description: Japan has booked its place in the Asian Cup semifinals with a hard-fought but ultimately sublime 7-0 victory over the Philippines at picture-perfect Stadium Australia.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli wins F1 Chinese GP from pole as Lewis Hamilton claims first Ferrari podium
item.description: Teenage dreams so hard to beat, and what a moment when realised for Kimi Antonelli as he took his first Formula One victory at the Chinese Grand Prix.

item.title: Formula One: Kimi Antonelli wins Chinese Grand Prix race updates – as it happened
item.description: But that’s about all that’s needed from me today.

item.title: Green records 'nerve-racking' Australian Open win to end 12-year drought
item.description: Hannah Green has won the Women's Australian Open by one shot in Adelaide.

item.title: Nepal’s uprising took their loved ones. Now they hope for change – gallery
item.description: Seventy-seven people were killed during youth-led protests that toppled Nepal’s prime minister Continue reading.

item.title: ‘No one saw this coming’: will the surprise Telegraph winner change the paper’s direction?
item.description: The day after Lord Rothermere was gazumped in his pursuit of the Telegraph by Axel Springer’s £575m knockout offer, the Daily Mail owner was pictured beaming at Rupert Murdoch’s 95th birthday party in New York.

item.title: Live: 'Half their salary cap gone': Knights' costly NRL win over Manly
item.description: The Dolphins storm back from 14-0 down to beat the Titans and become the first Queensland team to win a game this season.

item.title: Carlos Alcaraz stays positive after winning streak ended by Daniil Medvedev at Indian Wells
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz said he was finding it tough to constantly have a target on his back and was surprised by the level of performance from Daniil Medvedev after the Russian handed him his first defeat of the season at Indian Wells.

item.title: Live: Roos looking strong against Power as Demons begin life under new coach
item.description: North Melbourne plays host to a Port Adelaide side excited to begin life under new coach Josh Carr, in the first match of an AFL Sunday triple-header.

item.title: Race to restore 'raw horsepower' that gave outback motocross club true grit
item.description: An outback West Australian community is hoping to restore its motocross circuit and clubhouse, after it was handed over to the local shire.

item.title: Oscars 2026: What to expect, how to watch and who will win
item.description: Hollywood stars are gearing up for Sunday night's Oscars, in an exciting year for the awards race where several categories are too close to call.

item.title: Danish royals King Frederik and Queen Mary get rare treat at Uluru
item.description: Queen Mary and King Frederik of Denmark began the second day of their royal tour of Australia at a sacred waterhole at Uluru.

item.title: France win Six Nations with last kick as Thomas Ramos sinks England in thriller
item.description: This has been a stunningly unpredictable Six Nations and no one saw this remarkable final reel coming.

item.title: Three more Iranian women’s football squad members return home after being granted asylum in Australia
item.description: Three more members of the Iranian women’s football squad have left Australia, leaving behind teammates who sought asylum after playing in the Women’s Asian Cup.

item.title: Max Dowman breaks record as Arsenal boost title push with late win against Everton
item.description: It was the moment to blow the roof off the Emirates Stadium, the exclamation mark on a victory that felt pivotal to the destination of the Premier League title.

item.title: Rosenior insists no huddle ‘disrespect’ as Newcastle earn rare win at Chelsea
item.description: A first Newcastle victory at Stamford Bridge since 2012, Anthony Gordon their match-winner against the club he was close to joining in 2022.

item.title: How the Iranian-Australian community united to help footballers
item.description: Before Iran's women's football team even landed in Australia for the Women's Asian Cup, many members of the Iranian-Australian community were already coming together for a common cause.

item.title: Gatz review – the Great Gatsby performed in eight-and-a-half hours of attentive, immersive joy
item.description: A man enters his office in the morning, finds his computer on the fritz, and after a few attempts to turn it on and off again, comes across a copy of F Scott Fitzgerald’s 1925 novel The Great Gatsby.

item.title: 'On the run' or 'disfigured'? Mystery surrounds Iran's new supreme leader
item.description: Late last week, Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, broke his silence after days of intensifying rumours about his health and whereabouts, but it did little to put them to rest.

item.title: UK faces ‘seismic moment’ as nationalists target election wins, says John Swinney
item.description: The UK is facing an “absolutely seismic moment”, John Swinney has said, with the prospect of the election of first ministers in Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland in May who are all committed to the break-up of the union.

item.title: Reform UK government would replace top civil servants with policy ‘believers’
item.description: A Reform UK government would expect to dismiss the top civil servant in every government department and replace them with people seen as more likely to implement the party’s priorities, the Guardian has learned.

item.title: 'They honestly saved my life': The bikers helping abused kids feel safe
item.description: BACA members accompany kids to court, stand guard outside their homes, and provide a sense of safety with burly presences and deeply rumbling motorbikes.

item.title: Yankuba Minteh’s lucky strike earns Brighton first win at Sunderland for 45 years
item.description: Wearside will always be a special place for James Milner.

item.title: Swinney promises £500m childcare boost and more GP walk-in clinics
item.description: The first minister has pledged to spend more than £500m on boosting childcare provision if the SNP is re-elected in May.

item.title: Put your phone down and focus: the four best ways to watch movies
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: ‘You’ve got to be able to laugh at yourself’: Jamie Oliver stars in video for CMAT’s The Jamie Oliver Petrol Station
item.description: It is 27 years since Jamie Oliver first appeared on TV as The Naked Chef, bish-bash-boshing his way to cultural ubiquity thanks in part to his rock’n’roll credentials: his band Scarlet Division provided the show’s theme tune, and his love of Toploader made their cover of Dancing in the Moonlight a monumental hit.

item.title: Democratic lead in Georgia special election signals change in party’s fortunes
item.description: Earlier this week, a steady trickle of voters casting ballots in Dalton at Georgia’s City Hall offered a glimpse into what may be changing fortunes for Democrats in Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former congressional district.

item.title: Opinion: An ancient, sophisticated palate
item.description: We might look at cave paintings and conclude that our prehistoric ancestors speared a bison or saber-toothed tiger and just hefted their carcasses into a fire.

item.title: Master gardener Jessica Damiano explains seed packet lingo to aid successful gardening
item.description: Spring is just so close and with it - time to sow the seeds.

item.title: ‘As soon as I saw it, I knew the image’: Robby Ogilvie’s best phone picture
item.description: Ogilvie didn’t know it at the time, but the Ford Cortina in the shot is a familiar feature of the area, and is known affectionately as Cortie.

item.title: Reframing Georgia O'Keeffe's legacy and protecting the land she loved
item.description: ABIQUIU, NM – On a recent afternoon, fluffy clouds drift past the sun, throwing light, then shadow, across distant cliffs layered in yellow, ochre and sienna.

item.title: What links The Wonderful Wizard of Oz and Moonfleet? The Saturday quiz
item.description: 1 Which dictator spent his last days in power playing Candy Crush?2 What children’s TV production company was founded by Anne Wood?3 Rome’s Ludus Magnus was a training school for whom?4 Where were Liz Truss and Lord Salisbury both appointed prime minister?5 Referring to 17 metals, what does REE stand for?6 In the title of an 1886 novel, what is David Balfour’s predicament?7 Which fish has the scientific name Electrophorus electricus?8 What sport is the subject of the documentary Love Means Zero?What links: 9 Booker T & the MG’s; Manfred Mann; Ben Folds Five?10 Genoese; Lepers; Millionaires; Rat Stabbers; Red Devils?11 Moonfleet; Three Men in a Boat; The Wonderful Wizard of Oz; Consider Phlebas?12 Glengarry Glen Ross; Lawrence of Arabia; The Great Escape; 12 Angry Men?13 Arbuthnot Latham; Coutts; C Hoare; Weatherbys?14 Horn; Agulhas; Leeuwin; South East; Whiore?15 Nicholas Breakspear and Robert Prevost?.

item.title: Why Australia's famous flying doctors are more important now than ever before
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Zoo celebrates first UK-born elephant shrews
item.description: Two black and rufous sengi, known as elephant shrews, have been born for the first time in the UK.

item.title: Formula One: Chinese Grand Prix qualifying – live
item.description: What were the other major takeaways from our season-opening race in Melbourne?.

item.title: Six great reads: Louis Theroux’s reluctance to answer questions, Apple’s hits and misses, and boomers v gen Z
item.description: Lucia Osborne-Crowley has endured threats and sexual harassment to report on Jeffrey Epstein’s chief enabler.

item.title: Russell wins Shanghai sprint for Mercedes, Piastri finishes sixth
item.description: George Russell wins the Chinese Grand Prix sprint race ahead of Charles Leclerc and Lewis Hamilton.

item.title: Haiti's first Paralympic skier hits the slopes, as Australia's Ben Tudhope wins bronze
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: George Russell wins first F1 sprint race of season at Chinese Grand Prix
item.description: George Russell won the opening sprint race of the new Formula One season at the Chinese Grand Prix.

item.title: Love Island star Maya Jama ready to make the leap from presenter to actress
item.description: She might be one of the most recognisable faces on British TV as the host of Love Island and a panellist on The Masked Singer, but Maya Jama is ready to take the leap from presenter to leading lady.

item.title: What's in the free daytime power plan as global tensions rattle energy markets
item.description: The Australian Energy Regulator has released more details about the Government's plan to offer free power in the middle of the day for some Australians.

item.title: Biscuit, tiramisu and pistachio stuffing: dates just got even better
item.description: Lina Jebeile is a food photographer and content creator.

item.title: Crows claim impressive win over Pies, Swans demolish depleted Lions
item.description: Sydney has smashed a depleted Brisbane side at the SCG while Adelaide records an impressive victory over Collingwood at the MCG.

item.title: Slay the Spire 2 is a bit too familiar for its own good
item.description: Early Access impressions: New characters shine, but it feels like we’ve done this before.

item.title: Bennett calls fan invasion 'pretty disruptive' after historic Johnston try
item.description: South Sydney Rabbitohs coach Wayne Bennett seemed to have had enough of answering questions about Alex Johnston's NRL try-scoring record, during Friday's post-match press conference.

item.title: Mary and Fred's love story: How a girl from Hobart met a prince and became queen
item.description: King Frederik and Australian-born Queen Mary of Denmark are touring Australia this week.

item.title: A cholesterol test you've never heard of is now recommended to prevent heart disease
item.description: New guidelines for managing cholesterol call for more aggressive prevention and earlier treatment, including a recommendation that all adults be tested once for lipoprotein(a), a genetic risk marker for heart disease.

item.title: Vaccinating bats could be good for people. But how do you vaccinate a bat?
item.description: Here's a question you might never have asked yourself: How do you vaccinate a bat?.

item.title: Imperfect Matildas get perfect result to make Asian Cup semis
item.description: You only get one shot, or in this case two.

item.title: Perfect storm helping feral animals take over South Australia
item.description: A combination of drought conditions in South Australia and flooding in Queensland are contributing to a booming population of some feral animals.

item.title: Piastri pleased in China, but 'impressive' Mercedes continue F1 dominance
item.description: Mercedes has once again dominated the Formula 1 field, qualifying their cars first and second for Saturday's sprint race in China.

item.title: US airport security misses first paycheck as homeland security shutdown nears one month – live
item.description: Employees at the Transport Security Administration (Tsa) are set to miss their first full paychecks today as the partial shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) nears a month.

item.title: Process is progress as Scotland seek Six Nations title eliminator triumph
item.description: Trusting the process.

item.title: How Italy became the darlings (and contenders, too) of the World Baseball Classic
item.description: When a Team Italy player hits a home run, he dons an Armani jacket and takes a shot of espresso (and don't forget the kiss on each cheek from the team captain).

item.title: M5 MacBook Air review: Still the best MacBook for almost everybody
item.description: The M5 MacBook Air is a minor upgrade, but minor upgrades add up over time.

item.title: Great haul of China: how table-topping nation rose to be a Winter Paralympic power
item.description: With two days of competition left at the Winter Paralympics there is no doubt who will finish top of the medal table.

item.title: With $200m to spend on the midterms, crypto hopes to repeat its 2024 success: ‘It’s the most critical time’
item.description: With the first primaries of the US midterm elections now under way, the cryptocurrency industry is injecting millions of dollars into congressional races across the country, with particular emphasis on Illinois, which has attracted the bulk of the campaign financing.

item.title: 'I don't even think it was his message': Iranians on new supreme leader's first address
item.description: "I don't even think it was his message," an Iranian woman in her 40s told the BBC after her country's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei gave his first official address in the form of a statement read out on state TV.

item.title: ‘It works for me and it’s free!’: 18 tried-and-tested tips for better sleep
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Five Great Reads: the biggest jewelled egg in the world, the language that stumps Duo Lingo, and one thing you should do before you die
item.description: Good morning.

item.title: Cuba confirms recent talks with United States to resolve differences
item.description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel says his government has held recent talks with the United States, the first time Havana has confirmed such contacts.

item.title: 3G pitch given approval despite pollution fears
item.description: A new sports centre is set to be approved despite concerns about the risk of microplastic contamination from an artificial pitch.

item.title: The best recent science fiction, fantasy and horror – review roundup
item.description: The Library of Traumatic Memory by Neil Jordan (Head of Zeus, £20) Better known as a film-maker, Jordan has never stopped writing novels.

item.title: Add to playlist: the dadaist cubist racket of Angine de Poitrine and the week’s best new tracks
item.description: In 2023, two young men – their earthly identities a jealously guarded secret – began “a joke that spilled into reality” intended to simulate something like its namesake heart condition.

item.title: Musician Labrinth says he is 'done with this industry' and hits out at Euphoria
item.description: Musician Labrinth has distanced himself from TV series Euphoria, for which he composes the music, and said he is "done with this industry".

item.title: Mother honours 'beautiful boy' at rail station vigil attended by thousands
item.description: The mother of Aidan Becker, a 22-year-old who died trying to protect a teenage boy last Friday, has spoken of her family's deep pain during a packed community vigil for her son at a Melbourne railway station.

item.title: Radio 1 DJ Greg James sets off on 630-mile Comic Relief cycle challenge
item.description: Radio 1 DJ Greg James has set off on a tandem bike for his 630-mile cycle challenge for Comic Relief.

item.title: Less respawning, more re-rolling: six of the best board games based on video games
item.description: Video games have long been heavily inspired by physical games, from chess and Scrabble to Dungeons & Dragons.

item.title: Mother’s Day UK recipes: three delicious ideas to make for your mum from Ravinder Bhogal
item.description: Few things say “I love you” more than an unbidden cup of tea, but if you want to show true appreciation to the maternal figure in your life this Mother’s Day, there’s nothing better than a few indulgent snacks to go with it.

item.title: James Blake: Trying Times review – platitudes about politics and Kanye can’t detract from an excellent album
item.description: Of all the things you might expect from a James Blake album – exquisite minimalism, plaintive vocal distortion, appearances from hip-hop’s great and good – chin-stroking socio0political commentary probably isn’t one of them.

item.title: Dynasty: The Murdochs review – who cares which billionaire will control even more billions?
item.description: ‘To explain the Murdochs, you have to understand the television show Succession.” So quips New York Times writer Jim Rutenberg a few minutes into this four-part documentary about Rupert Murdoch’s empire – and, specifically, his children’s battle for control of it when he dies.

item.title: Why Marty Supreme should win the best picture Oscar
item.description: First things first: the best picture Oscar should go to Marty Supreme for the incredible job it has done in bringing new eyes to ping pong.

item.title: Gay Muslim influencer hosts inclusive Ramadan meal and calls for acceptance across faiths
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Ali Darwich, a gay Muslim influencer in Berlin, picks up a date from his plate, takes a sip of water, and addresses the 15 friends sitting around the table and breaking the Ramadan fast with him.

item.title: Woman with rare blood feels 'honoured' to donate
item.description: A woman whose blood donations are so rare they are frozen for up to 30 years said she feels "very special and honoured" to be able to help sick patients.

item.title: 'Get me out of here': Johnston says fans on the field was fun until it wasn't
item.description: Alex Johnston has become the greatest try-scorer in Australian rugby league history with a double against the Roosters, prompting fans to ignore the NRL's pleas not to storm the field to celebrate.

item.title: Benjamina Ebuehi’s recipe for caramelised white chocolate and rhubarb cheesecake | The sweet spot
item.description: It’s often my own impatience that forces me to make no-bake cheesecakes over baked ones.

item.title: Nepal election results - Balendra Shah's party wins in a landslide
item.description: The party of rapper and ex-Kathmandu mayor Balendra Shah has won a a clear majority, according to the final results from Nepal's general election held on 5 March.

item.title: Every Brilliant Thing review – Daniel Radcliffe sells tricky Broadway transfer
item.description: Every Brilliant Thing presents a theatrical gauntlet for Daniel Radcliffe, the erstwhile Harry Potter, acclaimed Broadway stage regular and only star of this 13-week limited engagement.

item.title: A young party led by a rapper wins a huge mandate - and Nepal steps into the unknown
item.description: The four-year-old Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP), led by rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah, has achieved a feat many thought impossible under Nepal's dual-election system: a sweeping majority.

item.title: Churchill's granddaughter happy with his picture being replaced on £5 note
item.description: Sir Winston Churchill will be replaced on the £5 note by an animal, the Bank of England has said.

item.title: Qantas told to 'be a good corporate citizen for once', as deal reached on flight credits
item.description: Qantas has agreed to settle a class action ​related to flight credits issued during the COVID-19 ‌pandemic.

item.title: Australians are right to ask ‘is this good enough?’ when it comes to the Nacc’s final robodebt report | A J Brown
item.description: Disappointed reactions to the National Anti-Corruption Commission’s long-awaited robodebt report say a lot about what many Australians have been expecting from the almost three-year-old Nacc.

item.title: French vote tests polarised electorate with right hoping to win control of Paris
item.description: France is preparing to vote in town council elections, whose results will be closely analysed for trends ahead of next year's presidential votes.

item.title: Winners, Sinners and record breakers: 17 fun facts about this year's Oscars
item.description: When Sinners director Ryan Coogler went to the movies as a child, he would smuggle in some snacks - and get particularly creative with the cinema's drinks machine.

item.title: Aussie curling underdogs set to take on world's best
item.description: The Australian women's curling team is competing at the world championships for the first time.

item.title: Loved one stuck inside the manosphere? Here's how to help
item.description: Researchers agree the manosphere can sow some dangerous seeds.

item.title: When the Matildas played in North Korea a full stadium laughed at them
item.description: In 2007 the Matildas found themselves on a bus travelling through empty streets of North Korea.

item.title: Swim rescue hero Austin Appelbee honoured by Irish president
item.description: Irish government minister Kevin Moran has presented WA teenager Austin Appelbee with gifts and a letter from the nation's president, Catherine Connolly.

item.title: Iran vows to keep Strait of Hormuz shut in first statement from new Supreme Leader
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader (Sort Of) Speaks Out
item.description:
Today, the new Iranian Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, gave his first statement pledging to continue blocking the Strait of Hormuz.
This was delivered not by Khamenei himself, but by a newsreader on Iranian state TV speaking over a photo of him, sparking further speculation as to his health and whereabouts.
Alex and James are joined by Jane Corbin, Panorama film maker, and Baran Abbasi, reporter and presenter for BBC Persian, to discuss what to make of the new Supreme Leader’s statement.
Plus, diplomatic correspondent, James Landale, joins Alex and James from Kyiv to analyse what the US-Israeli war with Iran means for the war in Ukraine and how President Zelensky and President Putin have responded.
You can now listen to Newscast on a smart speaker.

item.title: Weekly quiz: This dog was Best in Show at Crufts - what other prize did he win?
item.description: This week, Iran appointed a new supreme leader, a German city had to be evacuated when a large WW2 bomb was found, and the UK got a first listen to its unusual entry for this year's Eurovision song contest.

item.title: This tale of a Chicago school book ban was inspired by true events
item.description: I couldn't help but think of little Francie Nolan – who, like Smith, grew up in the tenements of Brooklyn in the early 20th century and aimed, as a young girl, to read every book she could find – as I tore through librarian Jarrett Dapier's debut young adult graphic novel, Wake Now in the Fire.

item.title: Special episode planned in The Archers tour
item.description: The Archers will mark its 75th anniversary with a national stage tour, it has been announced.

item.title: Centuries before the Inca, Peru's wealthy imported parrots from afar
item.description: The Inca Empire’s system of roads were built on centuries-old trade routes.

item.title: Medicare Advantage 'dark money' group tries to win higher payments for insurers
item.description: Judging by more than 16,300 comments recently posted on a federal government website, you'd think there was a groundswell of older Americans demanding that federal officials hike payments to their Medicare Advantage health insurance plans.

item.title: NFL free agency: Panthers, 49ers, Patriots, Jets and Rams are early winners
item.description: NFL teams have committed billions of dollars in free agency, hoping the players they’ve added can help them win a Super Bowl.

item.title: ‘Casting is a very easy thing for others to credit for’: Richard E Grant on cinema’s invisible moguls
item.description: This Sunday, casting directors will be honoured at the Oscars for the first time.

item.title: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah's party wins Nepal election
item.description: The Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) has won a landslide in Nepal's election - the first held since last year's youth-led protests toppled the then government.

item.title: The were dating AI partners when they found real love – with each other
item.description: People are reporting “dating” artificially intelligent companions – but not every relationship lasts.

item.title: Capital gains tax discount ‘overwhelmingly’ benefits investors in Australia’s richest electorates, analysis shows
item.description: Investors who live in the wealthy electorate of Wentworth in Sydney’s eastern suburbs claimed about $1.8bn from the 50% capital gains tax discount, according to new research.

item.title: Sydney Biennale 2026: politics is everywhere – but with nuance, beauty and heart
item.description: According to its critics, this year’s Biennale of Sydney, under the leadership of Emirati artistic director Hoor Al Qasimi (the first Arab appointed to the role in the festival’s 53-year history) was destined to be a “hate Israel jamboree” at worst; a hotbed of pro-Palestinian politics at best.

item.title: Chilean Smiljan Radić Clarke wins architecture's highest honor
item.description: Smiljan Radić Clarke was named the newest Pritzker Prize-winner — an award often called the Nobel of architecture — Thursday morning.

item.title: How do these grab you, darlin’? Nancy Sinatra’s 20 best songs – ranked!
item.description: Before she sang a Bond theme, Nancy Sinatra had recorded a parody of one: twanging guitar, John-Barry-mocking brass and all.

item.title: ‘Unbelievably unequal’: report shows how 1% of Mexicans own 40% of country’s wealth
item.description: Scrunched between luxury apartment buildings and a lush gated community, the neighborhood of Santa Lucía Reacomodo in Mexico City is a working-class pocket of real estate.

item.title: Save our soles! Cobblers on the 15 best ways to look after your shoes
item.description: Now that trainers have supplanted smart shoes for so many occasions, and people replace them as often as fashion and budget allow, shoe maintenance is becoming a lost art.

item.title: Why is smoking so addictive – and what are the best ways to give up?
item.description: Smoking is bad for you and you shouldn’t do it.

item.title: London, San Francisco and Beijing achieve ‘remarkable reductions’ in air pollution
item.description: London, San Francisco and Beijing are among 19 global cities that have achieved “remarkable reductions” in air pollution, analysis has found, having slashed levels of two airway-aggravating pollutants by more than 20% since 2010.

item.title: Why Sentimental Value should win the best picture Oscar
item.description: This is a best picture race full of ambitious ideas and big swings.

item.title: John Lewis to pay first staff bonus for four years
item.description: John Lewis is awarding its staff a bonus for the first time in four years as the retailer continues its turnaround.

item.title: Blues hang on for scratchy but vital win over Tigers at the MCG
item.description: Carlton has kicked only one goal in the second half, but it is just enough to hold off a late charge by a wayward Richmond at the MCG.

item.title: John Lewis pays first annual staff bonus in four years as profits rise
item.description: The owner of John Lewis and Waitrose has paid an annual bonus to workers for the first time in four years after underlying profits rose by 6%.

item.title: 'I imagine they had a healthy sex life early on' - Ruth Jones in Austen-inspired drama
item.description: Ruth Jones says she is "stepping into very big shoes" as she stars in a new BBC comedy series inspired by Jane Austen's Pride and Prejudice.

item.title: US orders petroleum reserve release in bid to calm markets – as it happened
item.description: Iraq’s oil ports have completely stopped operations while commercial ports continue to operate after an attack on a fuel tanker, an Iraqi official has said.

item.title: Fuel quality rules relaxed to add 100 million litres more petrol
item.description: The energy minister has relaxed fuel standards for two months in order for more supply to be brought into the domestic market.

item.title: Matt Canavan has promised me a hyper Australia. I’m ready to chug red cordial and get on board | James Colley
item.description: If you felt a sense of disillusionment with the world yesterday, if the star by which you navigate home disappeared from the sky, if sweet fruit turned to ash in your mouth and you wondered if anything would ever be beautiful again, you were not alone.

item.title: Darwin's boil water alert lifted after testing shows water is free of E.coli
item.description: The boil water alert is no longer in place for Darwin, after testing of water showed it was free of E.coli and other harmful bacteria.

item.title: As a refugee, I have lived in limbo for 13 years. Why are only some Iranian women worthy of Australia’s protection? | Fatemeh Lahmidi
item.description: When I heard this week that Australia had granted humanitarian visas to the Iranian women’s football team, I felt two very different emotions at once.

item.title: Australian champion leading new era of women's motocross in the US
item.description: Four-time Australian Champion Charli Cannon will feature in a new era of American motocross this year.

item.title: Google Play Games for PC is getting more premium titles and cross-buy with Android
item.description: Google has been tinkering with porting its Play Games platform to Windows for several years, but it started getting serious about it last year.

item.title: King penguins surfing on the 'good edge' of climate change — for now
item.description: King penguins on the Crozet Islands are breeding earlier and with a higher level of success than they did 24 years ago. Researchers say this is likely in response to their changing environment and food availability.

item.title: Captain Patterson guides NSW to One Day Cup win in rain-hit final
item.description: NSW has won the One Day Cup with a four-wicket win over Tasmania in the final.

item.title: Singapore has gone to great lengths to boost its fertility rate. Why hasn't it worked?
item.description: Boon Shang says if she decides to have children, she wants it to be for the right reasons.

item.title: Saunas' health benefits draw enthusiasts and researchers
item.description: Saunas are having a moment right now, thanks to a surge of interest in wellness.

item.title: Why IV drips are surging in popularity in Australia's wellness space
item.description: Bek Walter sits in a softly lit clinic in the Gold Coast's north while a bag of fluid slowly drips into her vein through a tube.

item.title: Defence town to choose between Huey and Seahawk for iconic landmark
item.description: The Department of Defence has opened a poll for Shoalhaven residents to choose whether the iconic chopper on a stick should feature a Huey or a Seahawk replica.

item.title: The best house shoes might be fuzzy Crocs. Yes, really
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Real Madrid v Manchester City: Champions League last 16, first leg – live
item.description: TNT Sports report that the Bernabeu isn’t sold out tonight.

item.title: Colts, QB Daniel Jones agree to 2-year deal worth up to $100 million, AP source says
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Quarterback Daniel Jones plans to stay with the Indianapolis Colts for at least two more years.

item.title: Bayer Leverkusen v Arsenal: Champions League last 16, first leg– live
item.description: Competitive balance across Europe’s leagues would be transformed with the adoption of a new model for distributing revenue from the Champions League and other Uefa club competitions, according to a proposal by the Union of European Clubs (UEC).

item.title: A clever person knows their limitations … Kemi believes she has none | John Crace
item.description: Taxi for Kemi.

item.title: More support as heating oil costs 'double'
item.description: Farmers and residents say they are concerned after heating oil costs have "doubled" since the conflict in Iran escalated.

item.title: NIH director launches "Scientific Freedom" lectures with non-scientist
item.description: First speaker minimizes climate change, COVID risks—and is a lab leak proponent.

item.title: Surely if you rule the manosphere, you can be your own boss? These influencers aren’t even that | Elle Hunt
item.description: Who wouldn’t want to be an influencer? You’re famous and maybe even rich, just for doing what you’d be doing anyway: working out at the gym, hanging out with your mates and mucking about on the internet.

item.title: Democrats deliver stunning flip in New Hampshire special election
item.description: A Democrat won a special election for a state house seat in New Hampshire on Tuesday, flipping a Republican district that Donald Trump carried and marking the latest in a string of 28 Democratic upsets that could usher in a blue wave in the midterms.

item.title: Becky the dog steals the show: John Dean’s best photograph
item.description: This was taken 50 years ago by my 20-year-old self.

item.title: Before sunrise: while the city sleeps, suhoor meals attract a lively social scene during Ramadan
item.description: It’s just after midnight in an industrial courtyard in Auburn in Sydney’s west and a glow of string lights and the constant sizzle of a grill signal one of Ramadan’s newest late-night rituals.

item.title: David Hockney review – a 90-metre vision of nature that only looks great on your phone
item.description: To call Hockney a gifted sentimentalist is no backhanded compliment.

item.title: Special relationships shape the 2026 Edinburgh International Festival
item.description: Nicola Benedetti fell in love with the United States when she was just 16 years old.

item.title: ‘Severe water stress’: why desalination plants are the Gulf’s greatest weakness
item.description: In 1983, the CIA determined that the most crucial commodity in the Gulf was its desalinated potable water.

item.title: The V&A’s Gilbert Galleries review – a fabulous treasure trove that must be seen to be believed
item.description: We periodically hear when a masterpiece is “saved for the nation”, usually when a museum is obliged to raise eye-watering sums to prevent the export or sale of an artwork deemed of national significance.

item.title: Sali Hughes on beauty: from blusher to lipstick – and even eyeshadow – a hint of cool purple adds polish
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: ‘When I leave, part of me stays’: why Scarborough’s youth won’t turn their backs on the seaside town they love
item.description: It’s the morning after a wet and stormy day in the Yorkshire seaside town of Scarborough.

item.title: G7 energy ministers say they support in principle use of strategic oil reserves – business live
item.description: News just in: the G7 group of nations said today that they supported, in principle, the implementation of proactive measures to address oil supply issues and market volatility, including the use of strategic oil reserves.

item.title: Why F1 the Movie should win the best picture Oscar
item.description: Could, should, would F1 the Movie win the best picture Oscar? Well, we have to be realistic here: F1 is currently a massive outsider, at 200-1 along with The Secret Agent, which has no chance either but for very different reasons.

item.title: The Iran oil crisis has proved Ed Miliband right on green energy. But households still need more help | Mathew Lawrence
item.description: Britain is once again paying the price of an energy system that is more effective at extracting profits than delivering security.

item.title: UK ticket holder wins £181m EuroMillions jackpot
item.description: A UK ticket holder has won Tuesday's £181m EuroMillions jackpot, the National Lottery operator has confirmed.

item.title: 'We deserve better after a year of all-out Birmingham bin strike'
item.description: It has been exactly a year since bin workers in Birmingham staged an all-out strike, with no current negotiations or seeming end in sight.

item.title: If plant-based foods must be more honest, let’s do the same for meat – fancy some ‘cow muscle’? | Deirdra Barr
item.description: Last week, European policymakers decided that plant-based foods should no longer be marketed with terms such as “chicken”, “bacon” or “steak”.

item.title: 'Everyone wants to know my true Kinky Boots story'
item.description: Everyone wants to know the "true story" of Kinky Boots, said the man who inspired the film that went on to become a hit musical.

item.title: The Perth Festival shows it's well and truly back post-COVID
item.description: After taking the reins in 2025, this year's Perth Festival was a chance for artistic director Anna Reece to consolidate the ideas she set in motion last year.

item.title: Joy prevails at WOMADelaide 2026
item.description: No matter who is playing, attending WOMADelaide tends to be an emotional experience.

item.title: Politics live: Spender says tax system ‘no longer fit for purpose’; Palmer vows to mail ads to all Australians as he announces run for parliament
item.description: Independent MP Allegra Spender says our tax system is “no longer fit for purpose”, as she releases a reform plan that would tax the owners of capital more and deliver tax relief for working Australians.

item.title: ‘My lovely distraction’: Live stream of kākāpō – world’s fattest parrot – and her chicks captivates New Zealand
item.description: On an island in New Zealand’s remote southern fjords, one of the world’s strangest and rarest parrots – the kākāpō – is caring for her tiny chick as fans from across the globe watch on.

item.title: Hawaii braces for powerful kona storm bringing heavy rain and strong winds
item.description: Hawaii is preparing for a powerful storm this week that is expected to cause intense winds, thunderstorms and possibly significant flooding across multiple islands.

item.title: Indigenous dance academy celebrates 50 years of cultural pride
item.description: Chetachi Nwaogiza has never felt more alive than when he is dancing.

item.title: Matt Canavan calls for 'hyper Australia' with 'more babies' as he leads National Party
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Childcare and more paid leave: Will China's new plan to boost spending work?
item.description: China's leaders are trying something new to revive the country's slowing economy: encouraging people to spend more.

item.title: NASA approved a safety waiver for this week's reentry of Van Allen Probe
item.description: A NASA satellite that spent more than a decade coursing through the Van Allen radiation belts encircling Earth is about to fall back into the atmosphere.

item.title: Two more Iranian football squad members granted humanitarian visas in Australia, minister confirms
item.description: A total of seven members of the Iranian women’s football team have now sought asylum in Australia, home affairs minister Tony Burke has confirmed.

item.title: Atlético 5-2 Tottenham, Newcastle 1-1 Barcelona: Champions League last-16 first legs – live
item.description: It was a night when the Tyneside passions pulsed; the nervous energy, too, because this was something unprecedented – a first Champions League knockout tie in Newcastle’s history.

item.title: Rapper Lil’ Kim to headline both Vivid Sydney and Melbourne’s 2026 Rising festival
item.description: The pioneering female rapper Lil’ Kim will headline both Vivid Sydney and Melbourne’s Rising this year, as each festival revealed its programs on Wednesday.

item.title: Musk’s xAI wins permit for datacenter’s makeshift power plant despite backlash
item.description: Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence company xAI won approval on Tuesday to run 41 methane gas turbines at its “Colossus 2” datacenter in northern Mississippi.

item.title: Happy birthday to the telephone — oh, how you've grown in 150 years!
item.description: These days, our phones are basically extensions of our bodies.

item.title: Why this popular dairy product has been flying off the shelf
item.description: Customers strolling through the refrigerated aisle at Australia's major supermarkets might notice a shortage of Greek yoghurt.

item.title: US weighs sending forces into Iran to secure nuclear stockpile, reports say
item.description: The Trump administration is reportedly considering the deployment of special forces into Iran to secure its stockpile of highly enriched uranium (HEU), which experts say could be used to make at least 10 nuclear warheads.

item.title: Philippines celebrate as jaw-dropping free kick lights up group stage finale
item.description: On a night that featured two high-stakes games played simultaneously on opposite sides of the country, it was a team that spent the evening huddled nervously around the television that ended it with the most to celebrate.

item.title: Galatasaray v Liverpool: Champions League last-16, first leg – live
item.description: As for the Galatasaray team … there is just one change to their starting XI from the 2-3 loss at Juventus last month.

item.title: Dr Xand on the wellness benefits of knitting
item.description: Listen to What's Up Docs? on BBc Sounds.

item.title: Ladies Day returns to Cheltenham festival promising ‘glamour and glory’
item.description: First and foremost, it is a huge sporting event, billed by its fans as the Olympics of jump racing – but it can also act as a social barometer, giving clues as to the state and mood of the nation.

item.title: Matthieu Blazy’s Chanel show celebrates and plays with brand’s history
item.description: A building site, but make it chic: that was the set for Chanel’s Paris fashion week show.

item.title: Perfect chopped chives are a status symbol for chefs. Can I learn to master ‘green confetti’?
item.description: My goal: a perfect 10 from Rate My Chives, the ‘number one authority on chives worldwide’.

item.title: Eni Aluko wins Joey Barton libel case over X posts
item.description: Former England footballer and pundit Eni Aluko has been awarded more than £300,000 damages and legal costs after suing Joey Barton for libel over social media posts.

item.title: Families welcome appointment of Donna Ockenden to Leeds maternity inquiry
item.description: Families who lost babies at two hospitals in Leeds have said they are slowly regaining trust in the health secretary after the midwife Donna Ockenden was appointed to lead a review into the failing service, where 56 babies and two mothers died in five years.

item.title: How a bid for freedom by Iran’s women footballers went deep into extra time
item.description: As the Philippines’ second goal sailed untouched into the back of the net, sealing their victory, the clock started ticking for their opponents: the Iranian women’s team were now out of the Asia Cup tournament.

item.title: Georgia voters to pick replacement for Marjorie Taylor Greene in special election
item.description: Her name isn't on the ballot.

item.title: Under financial strain, primary care doctors unite
item.description: Under increasing financial stress, primary care practices across the U.S.

item.title: Crowded field of candidates compete to replace MTG in Georgia special election
item.description: ATLANTA — Democrat Shawn Harris and Trump-endorsed Republican Clay Fuller will advance to an April runoff in a race to replace former Republican Rep.

item.title: ‘I’m buggered’: David Littleproud resigns as leader of National party as MPs jockey for top job
item.description: A crowded field of Nationals MPs are set to jockey for the leadership after a “buggered” David Littleproud blindsided colleagues with his shock resignation.

item.title: Iran expert discusses what new supreme leader means for country's future
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Iran expert Vali Nasr about what the appointment of a new Supreme Leader in Iran means for the country's future.

item.title: Why One Battle After Another should win the best picture Oscar
item.description: Viva la revolution and don’t forget your password, your pronouns, your plaid gown and your gun.

item.title: Super Netball's Kiwi influx leaves sour taste for diehard Australian fans
item.description: Super Netball's 10th season will have a Kiwi flavour to it and it's left a sour taste in the mouths of diehard Australian supporters.

item.title: 'Soft, friendly and some slobber': Crufts win puts Clumber spaniels in spotlight
item.description: The champion of Crufts, Bruin, is a breed you may not have heard of before - a Clumber spaniel.

item.title: Champions, captains and icons: The Iranian players granted asylum
item.description: Iran brought 26 players to Australia for the Women's Asian Cup and five of them have been granted asylum to avoid returning home.

item.title: New Zealand Covid response among world’s best but ‘scars’ remain, inquiry finds
item.description: A royal commission into New Zealand’s Covid response has found it was one of the best in the world but acknowledged the period had left “scars”.

item.title: Plan for thousands of new homes at 'one of Sydney's most vibrant areas'
item.description: Rezoning in Sydney's inner west could see thousands of new homes around an upcoming metro station.

item.title: NBA cancels team's plans to celebrate local Magic City strip club
item.description: The NBA has cancelled the Atlanta Hawks' plans to celebrate the Magic City strip club.

item.title: More whales are stranding. Now more people are needed to help them
item.description: More volunteers are being recruited to help rescue a growing number of whales, dolphins and porpoises washing up on UK shores.

item.title: Labor to pass super tax changes with support of Greens
item.description: Labor will pass legislation to increase the tax on those with super balances above $3 million, ending a years-long stalemate.

item.title: Rooster review – Steve Carell and a naked college president add wisdom to this cringe comedy drama
item.description: Humankind, as TS Eliot’s bird said in Burnt Norton , cannot bear too much reality.

item.title: Morning Mail: five Iranian footballers granted humanitarian visas, Hanson secured solar rebate, Wallabies get boost
item.description: Morning everyone.

item.title: Politics live: Australia to deploy aircraft to Gulf to protect civilians; PM praises ‘brave women’ of Iranian football team after visas granted
item.description: Donald Trump’s dramatic announcement about the granting by Australia of asylum to five Iranian women footballers was the big breaking news of the night.

item.title: Australia to send surveillance plane to help 'protect and secure' airspace in the Gulf
item.description: Australia will send a military aircraft and 85 ADF personnel to the United Arab Emirates to assist in the defence of Gulf nations that have been attacked by Iran.

item.title: ‘They are safe here’: five Iranian women footballers granted humanitarian visas in Australia, Burke confirms
item.description: Five members of the Iranian women’s football team have been granted humanitarian visas in Australia, with the federal government offering assistance to other players.

item.title: Ministers must act more quickly on deepfakes to protect women and girls, Kendall says
item.description: Ministers need to act more quickly to combat fast-changing threats from technology such as deepfakes, the technology secretary has said, as she warned about the risks women and girls face online.

item.title: Epstein paid for a conference of top scientists in 2006. His motives are now clear
item.description: Some 100 feet below the ocean's surface, Stephen Hawking peeked through the circular porthole of a submarine and saw the brilliant blue tropical water.

item.title: ‘Revolutionary’: Ukrainian para-biathlete wins silver using ChatGPT as his coach
item.description: Team Ukraine have hit the ground running at the Winter Paralympics, standing second in the medal table after three days of competition.

item.title: Top Arizona lawmaker says he's complied with a subpoena for 2020 election records
item.description: Arizona's state Senate president says he has complied with a subpoena he received last week seeking records from a flawed review of the 2020 election in Maricopa County.

item.title: What to know about Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader
item.description: Conflict in the Middle East has been escalating.

item.title: Will this year’s flu vaccine protect against the fast-moving Super- K strain?
item.description: Experts are urging Australians to roll up their sleeves as soon as the 2026 influenza vaccine becomes available, following last year's low vaccine uptake and high caseload.

item.title: Individual grit won’t make men beautiful | Letters
item.description: Your piece on the rise of impossible male beauty standards (‘There is no shame in being vain’: the relentless rise of impossible male beauty standards, 5 March) captures something bigger than vanity, that of a neoliberal moral economy which turns the body into a private “project” and then invoices the individual for failing it.

item.title: SEND reforms to 'strip away' children's legal protections, charity says
item.description: The government's plans to reform the special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) system in England will "strip away" legal protections for children and young people needing support, a charity has warned.

item.title: No. 2 UCLA ends UConn run as unanimous No. 1 in women’s AP Top 25, Texas moves up to No. 3
item.description: No.

item.title: 2026 Australian Grand Prix: Formula 1 debuts a new style of racing
item.description: The key is understanding how to conserve energy across a lap.

item.title: Richest super balances to be taxed at higher rates after Greens agree to back Labor plan
item.description: Labor’s plan to raise the tax rate on the largest super balances will become law after the Greens agreed to wave it through the Senate, ending a three-year political fight over concessions for wealthier retirees.

item.title: ‘Bitter result’ for Friedrich Merz as Greens win in German car heartland
item.description: Friedrich Merz’s Christian Democrats (CDU) have stumbled into a busy election year with a defeat to the Greens in a key state poll, as his embattled party struggles to fend off a challenge in other pivotal races from the far right.

item.title: 'Welcome to stay': Five Iranian football players granted asylum in Australia
item.description: Five Iranian soccer players, who would likely have faced persecution if they returned home after their Women's Asian Cup campaign, have been granted asylum in Australia.

item.title: Georgina Hayden’s quick and easy recipe for cauliflower, lentils and chorizo | Quick and easy
item.description: The transformation that cauliflower undergoes in a very hot oven means there is now rarely a time when I don’t roast it first.

item.title: Ali Khamenei’s son Mojtaba chosen as Iran’s new supreme leader | First Thing
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, the second son of the late Iranian supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has been chosen as his successor, as the war enters its 10th day and fresh missile and drone strikes reverberate across the Middle East.

item.title: Chimps' taste for fermented fruit hints at origins of human love of alcohol
item.description: For 11 days in late summer 2025, Aleksey Maro found himself in the Ugandan rainforest, doing whatever he could to collect chimpanzee urine.
